2603.23319.title: WISTERIA: Weak Implicit Signal-based Temporal Relation Extraction with Attention
2603.23319.paper_summary: Temporal Relation Extraction (TRE) requires identifying how two events or temporal expressions are related in time. Existing attention-based models often highlight globally salient tokens but overlook the pair-specific cues that actually determine the temporal relation. We propose WISTERIA (Weak Implicit Signal-based Temporal Relation Extraction with Attention), a framework that examines whether the top-K attention components conditioned on each event pair truly encode interpretable evidence for temporal classification. Unlike prior works assuming explicit markers such as before, after, or when, WISTERIA considers signals as any lexical, syntactic, or morphological element implicitly expressing temporal order. By combining multi-head attention with pair-conditioned top-K pooling, the model isolates the most informative contextual tokens for each pair. We conduct extensive experiments on TimeBank-Dense, MATRES, TDDMan, and TDDAuto, including linguistic analyses of top-K tokens. Results show that WISTERIA achieves competitive accuracy and reveals pair-level rationales aligned with temporal linguistic cues, offering a localized and interpretable view of temporal reasoning.
2603.23319.paper_summary_zh: 時間關係提取（TRE）需要識別兩個事件或時間表達式在時間上的關係。現有的基於注意力的模型通常強調全局顯著的標記，但忽略了實際決定時間關係的特定於對的線索。我們提出了 WISTERIA（基於弱隱式信號的時間關係提取與注意力），這是一個框架，用於檢查每對事件條件下的前 K 個注意力組件是否真正編碼可解釋的時間分類證據。與之前的工作假設明確的標記如「之前」、「之後」或「何時」不同，WISTERIA 將信號視為任何隱式表達時間順序的詞彙、句法或形態元素。通過將多頭注意力與對條件的前 K 個池化相結合，該模型為每對隔離出最具信息量的上下文標記。我們在 TimeBank-Dense、MATRES、TDDMan 和 TDDAuto 上進行了廣泛的實驗，包括對前 K 個標記的語言分析。結果顯示 WISTERIA 實現了具有競爭力的準確性，並揭示了與時間語言線索對齊的對級理由，提供了時間推理的局部和可解釋的視角。
2603.23319.author: Duy Dao Do et.al.
2603.23319.authors: Duy Dao Do, Anaïs Halftermeyer, Thi-Bich-Hanh Dao
